dataBoundary: Reusable demo-underwriting facts. Visual/OCR/financial findings are deterministic prototype detector outputs and are explained by local Qwen or fallback narratives.

cases.scenario: Tampered Karnataka 7/12 land record detected before agricultural loan disbursement.
ingestionSteps: Classifying uploaded 7/12 extract and mutation chain.
Extracting survey number, owner chain, area and stamp fields.
Cross-referencing with demo Karnataka Bhoomi-style land reference.
Running pixel-level edit, stamp watermark and OCR consistency checks.
Packing cited anomaly evidence for local Qwen explanation.

anomalies.title: Inserted survey-number digit
anomalies.why: The digit group in survey number 142/1B has scan noise and edge sharpness inconsistent with the surrounding revenue-record text.
anomalies.observed: Digit 4 and suffix B are 2.7x sharper than neighboring glyphs and sit 3 pixels above the baseline.
anomalies.baseline: Demo Bhoomi-style reference expects survey number 142/1A for property BHOOMI-HBL-142-1A.

anomalies.title: Land area mismatch
anomalies.why: The uploaded record says 2.4 acres, while the demo registry polygon reference stores 1.8 acres for the same owner-chain.
anomalies.observed: Uploaded area: 2.4 acres; registry reference: 1.8 acres; variance: 33.3%.
anomalies.baseline: Area variance above 10% requires physical registrar verification in this prototype profile.

anomalies.title: Revenue stamp micro-text missing
anomalies.why: Stamp border lacks the micro-printing texture expected in the reference stamp family and appears copied from a lower-resolution scan.
anomalies.observed: Stamp watermark band has flat luminance and no alternating micro-line pattern.
anomalies.baseline: Reference stamp specimen contains repeating micro-text around the seal boundary.

riskDecomposition.label: Source Trustworthiness
drivers: Registry corroboration is demo-local and needs field verification

cases.memo: This agricultural land mortgage file shows probable tampering in the survey-number and area fields of the 7/12 extract, with missing stamp micro-text. Recommended action: escalate to branch manager and district registrar for physical verification before disbursement.
nextActions: Freeze disbursement until original land record is physically verified.
Ask branch officer to collect registrar-certified extract for survey 142/1A.
Record human override only with supporting field-verification note.

cases.scenario: Secured MSME term loan with collateral and statement anomalies.
ingestionSteps: Classifying sale deed, encumbrance extract and statement pages.
Extracting borrower, survey, date and account-flow entities.
Comparing statement rhythm with demo MSME sector baseline.
Checking collateral text against extracted encumbrance fields.
Preparing Qwen-citable underwriting memo.

anomalies.title: Execution-date edit signature
anomalies.why: The execution date region has a newer edit timestamp and a mismatched raster-noise field compared with the rest of the deed.
anomalies.observed: Date field compression artifacts are 41% denser than the surrounding clause text.
anomalies.baseline: Sale deed date should match scan noise and metadata of the page body.

anomalies.title: Survey number conflicts with encumbrance text
anomalies.why: The encumbrance extract and sale deed resolve to different survey identifiers for the same collateral narrative.
anomalies.observed: Sale deed mentions 88/2C; encumbrance text resolves as 88/2G after OCR correction.
anomalies.baseline: Collateral survey identifier must remain identical across sale deed, EC and valuation report.

anomalies.title: Unexplained working-capital inflow spike
anomalies.why: The statement shows a Month 9 deposit pattern outside the demo sector baseline and overlaps with high-risk counterparty flow paths.
anomalies.observed: Inflow spike is 4.2 standard deviations above the infrastructure-contractor baseline.
anomalies.baseline: Demo MSME baseline expects stable monthly inflow with sigma limit 3.2.

riskDecomposition.label: Source Trustworthiness
drivers: Source corroboration has stale public-source pressure

cases.memo: This secured MSME file combines collateral inconsistency, sale deed edit evidence and abnormal statement inflow. Recommended action: hold working-capital decision and escalate collateral plus fund-flow review.
nextActions: Request original sale deed and registrar-certified EC.
Ask borrower to explain the abnormal statement inflow with invoices and GST trail.
Use 3D fund-flow view to review counterparties before credit sanction.

cases.scenario: MSME working-capital enhancement with suspected circular deposits.
ingestionSteps: Classifying bank statement and GST summary.
Extracting monthly deposits, parties and GST turnover.
Running circular-flow and related-party overlap checks.
Preparing explainable financial anomaly cards.

anomalies.title: Circular deposit pattern
anomalies.why: A high-value deposit returns through a supplier account sharing address and timing overlap with the applicant.
anomalies.observed: Deposit spike is 6.3 sigma above the food-processing baseline and reverses within 48 hours.
anomalies.baseline: Expected monthly deposits remain within 3 sigma for this segment and region.

riskDecomposition.label: Document Authenticity
drivers: Statement template acceptable but needs original file

riskDecomposition.label: Data Veracity
drivers: GST turnover exceeds bank inflow rhythm

cases.memo: The MSME statement contains a high-confidence circular deposit pattern that materially changes working-capital eligibility. Recommended action: hold enhancement and request GST-linked invoices plus counterparty confirmation.
nextActions: Request invoice trail for spike month.
Run Qwen explanation on selected fund path.

cases.scenario: Property-backed overdraft with legal opinion seal and witness concerns.
ingestionSteps: Classifying legal opinion and seal crop.
Extracting witness, advocate and property identifiers.
Comparing seal and signature regions with demo specimen vault.
Preparing legal-risk explanation cards.

anomalies.title: Advocate seal compression mismatch
anomalies.why: The seal region has a different compression field and lacks continuous circular ink spread.
anomalies.observed: Seal boundary has clipped edges and repeated block artifacts.
anomalies.baseline: Demo advocate seal specimen expects continuous circular edge and medium ink spread.

cases.memo: The legal opinion has a seal-region anomaly and witness-source concern. Recommended action: request advocate confirmation and original stamped opinion before overdraft sanction.
nextActions: Ask legal risk desk to verify advocate seal.
Document reviewer conclusion in audit trail.

cases.scenario: Retail home loan with minor resolved KYC spacing issue.
ingestionSteps: Classifying application and income proof.
Normalizing name spacing and KYC aliases.
Checking income continuity and account rhythm.
Preparing low-risk explanation summary.

anomalies.title: PAN name spacing normalized
anomalies.why: A minor spacing mismatch is resolved by alias normalization and does not change underwriting risk materially.
anomalies.observed: Name spacing differs by one whitespace token.
anomalies.baseline: PAN and application name normalize to the same applicant identity.

cases.memo: This retail home-loan file has no material anomaly. The only data issue is a resolved PAN spacing mismatch. Recommended action: proceed with standard underwriting controls.
nextActions: Keep source watcher active until sanction package is closed.
